channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Judiciary Maintains Court Operations Amid Ongoing Boycott
item.description: The Judiciary has confirmed that court services are proceeding without interruption across all stations in Kenya despite a nationwide boycott by legal professionals. In a statement released on July 22...

item.title: Trump's Influence on Sports and Politics: A Pattern of Corruption
item.description: The article critiques former President Donald Trump's alleged influence over various incidents, highlighting how his connections with figures like Tiger Woods, the World Cup red card controversy, and ...

item.title: Four-Year-Old from B.C. Okanagan Missing; Amber Alert Issued
item.description: A four-year-old child from British Columbia’s Okanagan region is missing, prompting the issuance of an Amber Alert by local authorities. The alert was announced on July 22, 2026, following reports of ...

item.title: FINEX Advocates for Lower Input Costs to Offset Wage Hikes and Maintain Business Viability
item.description: The article discusses FINEX's assertion that wage hikes in the Philippines must be accompanied by reduced input costs to ensure businesses remain competitive and sustainable. With rising operational e...

item.title: Wildfire forces evacuation of 400 people in southern France
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire erupted in southern France, forcing hundreds to flee their homes as nearly 1,000 firefighters and aerial units battled to contain the blaze. The fire swept through wooded hills ...

item.title: Andrew Charlton's Role in Shaping Australia's AI Policy
item.description: Andrew Charlton, a junior minister in Australia's Labor government, is emerging as a key figure in shaping the nation's approach to artificial intelligence. With a strong academic background in econom...

item.title: Tragedy in Haldwani: Woman and Two Sons Die in Water Tank Incident
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Haldwani's Gaulapar area on Tuesday evening when a 65-year-old woman and her two sons died of suspected asphyxiation after entering a newly constructed underground water ...

item.title: Delhi Police Register FIR After Assault on RAF Personnel During CJP Protests
item.description: The Delhi Police have registered a first information report (FIR) following alleged assaults on two Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel by protesters during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) march. Deput...

item.title: Thabo Mbeki criticizes South Africa's lack of direction on inequality and poverty
item.description: Former President Thabo Mbeki has warned that South Africa lacks a coherent strategy to address deepening inequality, poverty, and unemployment, calling the country 'leaderless' in its efforts. Speakin...

item.title: Innovation Hubs to Drive Technological and Economic Growth in Tanzania
item.description: The article discusses the growing importance of innovation hubs in Tanzania's development landscape. These hubs are positioned to play a pivotal role in fostering technological advancement, business i...

item.title: Kelly Osbourne Reflects on Grief a Year After Ozzy's Passing
item.description: Kelly Osbourne has shared profound reflections on the enduring pain of grief one year after the death of her father, the legendary rock icon Ozzy Osbourne. The article details her emotional journey, d...

item.title: Arizona Republicans Elect 2020 Deniers and Trump Backers
item.description: Arizona Republicans have elected a slate of candidates who deny the 2020 presidential election results and openly support former President Donald Trump. This decision reflects the growing influence of...

item.title: Philippines Condemns Chinese Coast Guard Attack on Navy Personnel, Marcos Meets Envoy
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) confirmed that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. conveyed the Philippines' protest against the Chinese Coast Guard's assault on a Filipino Navy member to Chinese A...

item.title: Ferry Capsizes Off Guyana Coast, Leaving 41 Dead and Dozens Missing
item.description: A ferry carrying 179 passengers capsized off the coast of Guyana, resulting in at least 41 deaths and dozens still missing. The MV Barima, built in 1939, was en route from Georgetown to Port Kaituma w...

item.title: Two farmers kidnapped by gunmen in Ondo State
item.description: Two farmers were kidnapped by suspected gunmen in the Oke-Ifira/Idogun forest area of Akoko South-East Local Government in Ondo State. A relative of one victim reported the incident to police and Amot...

item.title: S'dumo Mtshali Shares Real-Life Impact of Playing Despised Character Jonasi in 'The Polygamist'
item.description: S'dumo Mtshali, the South African actor playing villain Janasi Gomora in Netflix drama *The Polygamist*, has opened up about the challenges of portraying a deeply flawed character. During a CNN interv...

item.title: Father of crypto casino mogul's mansion destroyed in suspicious fire
item.description: A $16 million mansion in Coffs Harbour, owned by the father of crypto casino billionaire Ed Craven, has been destroyed in a fire deemed 'suspicious' by police. The incident, which occurred on July 22,...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh passes Uniform Civil Code bill to ban polygamy and regulate live-in relationships
item.description: The Madhya Pradesh Assembly has passed the Uniform Civil Code Bill, aiming to ban polygamy and introduce strict regulations for live-in relationships. The bill, introduced by the BJP-led government, s...

item.title: High Court to Rule on President Ramaphosa's Impeachment Case
item.description: The Western Cape High Court is set to deliver its judgment on Friday regarding President Cyril Ramaphosa's urgent application to interdict the Section 89 committee investigating the Phala Phala scanda...
